event.title: Musical Potluck
event.description_text:
Join us for an afternoon of music, followed by a potluck supper. Musicians of all ages and skill levels are invited to participate, with opportunities to play in both small and large groups. Our goal is to bring together Oberlin’s vibrant musical community for a fun and welcoming experience.

If you don’t play an instrument, you’re still eagerly invited—come relax and enjoy a wide variety of live music across genres. Please RSVP.

event.title: The Oberlin Theatre Connected Universe
event.description_text: A special presentation that dares to ask the question: What if EVERY play performed on campus this year...was connected in a single world? Covering more than 20 shows (department, student-run, and everything in-between), this totally serious academic lecture presented by Robin Oliveira ’28 weaves together a massive story of gods and monsters, Wet Skeletons and Great Comets, and more spacetime-warping conspiracy than you could possibly imagine.

event.title: Concert: Marlene Rosen Memorial Tribute
event.description_text:
Watch the webcast  – streamed live at concert time.

Oberlin Conservatory of Music hosts a memorial tribute concert for beloved voice faculty member of 40 years, Marlene Rosen, in Warner Concert Hall featuring performances and remarks by former students and colleagues. A reception will immediately follow the concert in the Warner Lobby. Read Oberlin's remembrance of Marlene Rosen.

Welcome
Dean William Quillen

Tributes
Salvatore Champagne ’85
Rev. Dr. Alisha Lola Jones ’03
Timothy Weiss

Program

Sergei Rachmaninoff Сон, Op. 38, No. 5 (Sologub)
  Magdalena Kuźma ’18
  Thomas Bandy, piano

Francis Poulenc Sanglots from Banalités (Apollinaire)
  Colette Boudreaux ’06
  Daniel Michalak, piano

Richard Strauss Beim Schlafengehenfrom Vier letzte Lieder (Hesse)
  Aubry Ballaro ’14
  Thomas Bandy, piano

Samuel Barber St. Ita’s Vision from Hermit Songs
  Amber Rogers ’23
  Daniel Michalak, piano

Richard Strauss September from Vier letzte Lieder (Hesse)
  Jennifer Noel ’07
  Daniel Michalak, piano

Claude Debussy Apparition from Quatre chansons de jeunesse (Mallarmé)
  Heidi Middendorf ’15
  Thomas Bandy, piano

Artie Butler Here’s to Life (Molinary)
  Tamara Jade Fingal ’11
  Daniel Michalak, piano

_____

Conservatory visitors are asked to enter the complex through either Bibbins Hall’s east entrance (off College Place) or the Conservatory Lounge’s west entrance (off S. Professor St., adjacent to the Conservatory Pond). All other entrances will be closed to the public. Restrooms available in Bibbins Hall.

event.title: F 1RST Things First: College Finances
event.description_text:
Join the Center for Student Success and other campus partners for the third session of this six-part virtual series that will prepare incoming students for personal and academic success at Oberlin! Students participating in this session will gain a deeper understanding of financial aid and will explore other strategies for navigating collegiate finances. 

All incoming students are welcome to attend.

Please pre-register.

event.title: Tuesday Tea / Bad Art? Engaging Students with the Unexpected and the Unconventional
event.description_text: How can we teach with art that refuses categorization? Fiona Giménez-Collins (OC 2025), Academic Programs Curatorial Assistant, will discuss sparking student dialogue around art objects that challenge typical modes of viewing. Delving into experiences of teaching with Mary Miss's Untitled (1975), Giménez-Collins will reflect on pushing students past discomfort and into new understandings of unconventional artworks.

event.title: In a Different Key: Pursuing a Calling Beyond the Paycheck
event.description_text: How do musicians pursue music as a calling when it is not their job? This talk explores the freedom, meaning, and loss that come with keeping art separate from income. This special event is sponsored by the Business Program and Conservatory Professional Development Office. Pizza will be served.

event.title: Great Lakes Theater & Oberlin present Kander & Ebb's The World Goes 'Round
event.description_text:
Oberlin Conservatory's Music Theater department presents Kander & Ebb's The World Goes 'Round at Playhouse Square's Hanna Theatre, as part of partnership with Great Lakes Theater.

Show times are Saturday, May 16 at 7:30pm and Sunday, May 17 at 3pm. These shows are in-person only. Tickets are required via the Box Office at Playhouse Square. General admission seating.

The World Goes ’Round
Music by John Kander
Lyrics by Fred Ebb
Conceived by Scott Ellis, Susan Stroman, & David Thompson

Alex Sanchez, director & choreographer
Dale Rieling, music director

The World Goes 'Round is a stunning revue of the songbook from the multi-Tony award-winning team, Kander and Ebb, with the original production winning three Drama Desk Awards.

Filled with humor, romance, drama and nonstop melody, The World Goes ’Round is a thrilling celebration of life and the fighting spirit that keeps us all going. From Cabaret to Chicago, the nonstop hit-parade features unforgettable gems, including Mr. Cellophane, Maybe This Time, Cabaret and New York, New York, seamlessly interwoven into a passionate, harmonious, up-tempo evening of musical theatre.

event.title: OFS Presents "The Graduate" (1967)
event.description_text: The Oberlin Film Society (OFS) will screen The Graduate (1967), a comedy-drama directed by Mike Nichols. Starring Dustin Hoffman, the film captures post-college aimlessness and generational anxiety in 1960s America. With its iconic soundtrack and sharp humor, it helped launch the New Hollywood era.

event.title: Unity Celebration
event.location_name: John W. Heisman Club Field House
event.description_text:
The Multicultural Resource Commons (MRC) is excited to invite pending graduates to participate in the annual Unity Celebration. This celebration at Oberlin College and Conservatory honors and celebrates the achievements of students of color and their allies/supporters.

This year, the Unity Celebration will occur on Thursday, May 21, from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. in the Heisman Field House. If you are attending, arrive no later than 9:40 a.m. so we may start on time.

Important Notes:

To participate in the Unity Celebration, including being recognized in the slideshow and program and being considered for student speaker or performer, you MUST register before Friday, May 1. Use this form to register.Those who do not register by May 1 are still welcome to join in on either event but will not be individually recognized nor considered to be a keynote speaker or performer.The Unity Celebration will include:

A speech by a graduating studentPerformance(s) by graduating student(s)Individual recognition of participating graduatesRefreshments and photo opportunitiesStudents, faculty, and staff are warmly welcome to attend. To ensure a supportive and affirming environment, we ask that any guests, including friends and family, be personally invited by a graduating student participating in the event. All are welcome. Use this form to RSVP.

Questions? Please send us an email at mrc@oberlin.edu.

event.title: Guided Tour of the Jewett House by Fanny Jewett
event.ticket_cost: $6/adult; $3/college student; and free for children under 18
event.description_text: Learn about Frank Fanning Jewett, Sarah Frances (Fanny) Gulick Jewett, Charles Martin Hall, the Aluminum Reduction Process and life in Oberlin during the Progressive Era on this 45-minute guided tour of the Oberlin Heritage Center’s 1884 Jewett House. Additional topics include architecture, interior design, social reform, and student boarders who stayed with the family during their time at Oberlin College. This experience includes a costumed living history portrayal of Fanny Jewett. Cost: $6/adult; $3/college student; and free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 14 participants. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

event.title: Tour: Freedom’s Friends: Abolition & the Underground Railroad History Walk
event.description_text:
Prior to the Civil War, as many has 3,000 African Americans passed through or lived in Oberlin after escaping from slavery. The town was said to be second only to Canada as an asylum for freedom seekers. Guests on this guided history walk offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center will hear about the historic decision that shaped Oberlin’s growth as a station on the Underground Railroad and stories of the individuals and events that marked Oberlin as one of the most active abolitionist communities in Ohio.

Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 50 participants. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

event.title: Multifaith End-of-Year Ceremony
event.description_text:
Students, faculty, staff, friends, and family are warmly invited to a joyful multifaith (including non-theistic) end-of-year ceremony as we send off the class of 2026.

At this gathering, students will share readings and music that are meaningful to them, drawn from many traditions, cultures, and personal perspectives. Faculty will also offer letters of wisdom and encouragement as graduates prepare for what's next. This year, we are excited to hear letters written and read by Courtney-Savali Andrews, Raphael Jiménez, Greggor Mattson, John Petersen, and Ellen Wurtzel!

Whether you are religious/spiritual or not, you are absolutely welcome here. This is a space for everyone to pause, reflect, and celebrate the community we've built together, and most especially the candidates for graduation.

Family members, loved ones, and Oberlin community members encouraged to attend.

Come as you are and join us for a ceremony of reflection and celebration.

event.title: Tour: Architecture History Walk
event.description_text:
Enjoy a leisurely stroll on this 75-minute tour offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center which explores Oberlin’s architecture and the people who shaped the area around Tappan Square. This walk provides an opportunity to view an array of historic buildings that feature various styles including Gothic Modernist, Neo-Classical Revival, Romanesque, Late Victorian, and Queen Anne Revival.

Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 20 participants. Meet at the flagpoles on the southeast corner of Tappan Square, across from the Hotel at Oberlin. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

event.title: Tour: Civil War to Civil Rights History Walk
event.description_text:
This 90-minute tour offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center highlights historic events that reveal both Oberlin’s progress and setbacks in race relations dating from early Oberlin all the way up to the 21st Century. Learn how Oberlin was a leader in abolition, participating heavily in events such as the Oberlin-Wellington Rescue, the Raid on Harpers Ferry, and the American Civil War, but also struggled with its own issues of equality and racism during these early years and later through Reconstruction, Jim Crow, Civil Rights, and today.

Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 40 participants. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

event.title: Oberlin Shansi Breakfast
event.description_text: The Oberlin Shansi team is thrilled to announce that we will be holding the Shansi Breakfast at this year’s Oberlin Commencement and Reunion Weekend on Sunday, May 24, 2026 from 8:30 a.m. to 10 a.m. in Biggs Commons, Stevenson Dining Hall. Shansi alumni, friends, new Fellows and their families, and reunion attendees interested in Shansi’s efforts to connect Oberlin and Asia are all welcome. The Shansi Breakfast connects generations as we introduce our newest cohort of Shansi Fellows to the wider community, welcome alumni to reconnect, and bring you up to date on what Shansi has been up to over the last year. Additionally, we’ll have time to tell stories, share a meal, and (of course) enjoy some strawberries and cream. Please join us to celebrate Shansi tradition and look to the future as we welcome a new group of Shansi Fellows preparing to head abroad.

event.title: Tour: One Step More: Oberlin Women’s History Walk
event.description_text:
In 1834, John J. Shipherd pledged Oberlin’s commitment to “the elevation of female character” through education. Little did he know, there was a dynamite in that promise, and women carried the matches. This 75-minute walking tour offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center explores how powerful women made good on Shipherd’s promise and shaped what it meant to be a woman in Oberlin and beyond. This tour was developed by alumna Jen Graham ’12.

Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 20 participants. Meet at the flagpoles on the southeast corner of Tappan Square, across from the Hotel at Oberlin. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

event.title: Frank Lloyd Wright Open House: Commencement and Milestone Reunion Weekend
event.ticket_cost: $10 per adult, free for OC students with ID and youths under 18.
event.description_text:
The Weltzheimer/Johnson House, designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, will be available for a walk-up open house. Presentations on the history of the home will be offered on the half hour. No advance tickets are required for this event and can be purchased at the door; cash only—$10 for adults (all funds raised contribute to the care of conservation of the house); OC students (with ID) and youths under 18 are free.

PARKING: Parking is available on the south side of Morgan Street only (golf course side). Permission to park in this location for the open house is granted by the Oberlin Police Department.

ARRIVAL: After parking, please follow the gravel path down and around the house to check in under the carport.

ACCESSIBILITY: Please consult the Frank Lloyd Wright House page on the Allen Memorial Art Museum’s website for accessibility information or contact the AMAM Education Department if you have any questions.

event.title: Tour: Freedom’s Friends: Abolition & the Underground Railroad History Walk
event.description_text:
Prior to the Civil War, as many has 3,000 African Americans passed through or lived in Oberlin after escaping from slavery. The town was said to be second only to Canada as an asylum for freedom seekers. Guests on this guided history walk offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center will hear about the historic decision that shaped Oberlin’s growth as a station on the Underground Railroad and stories of the individuals and events that marked Oberlin as one of the most active abolitionist communities in Ohio.

Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 50 participants. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

event.title: Black Families Appreciation Ceremony
event.description_text: The Black Families Appreciation Ceremony is an annual pre-commencement ceremony and dinner. This annual event allows self-identifying Black graduates and allies the opportunity to express words of gratitude in front of their community and receive their kente cloth stole. This event is for graduating students, their families and friends, faculty and staff, and community supporters. Dinner is served immediately following the ceremony at Afrikan Heritage House. All are welcome to participate.

We will honor the graduates of the Class of 2026 with a Commencement Ceremony on Tappan Square. The ceremony begins with a faculty-led procession followed by presentations and conferring of degrees.

The event is outdoors and will take place rain or shine. No tickets are required.

event.title: Frank Lloyd Wright Open House / June-November 2026
event.ticket_cost: $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18
event.description_text:
Designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, the Weltzheimer/Johnson House is the first Usonian house in Ohio, and one of the few in the nation open to the public. Situated a mile and a half from the art museum at 534 Morgan Street in Oberlin, the house is open to the public the first Sunday of the month (with the exception of holiday weekends) from April to November with a docent presentation offered on the hour at noon, 1, 2, 3, and 4 p.m.

Advance registration is required. Admission to the house is $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18. For tickets, parking information, and other details, see amam.oberlin.edu/flw.

Make a day of it!
The Allen Memorial Art Museum is open 1–5 on Sundays, and is always free. Plus, enjoy 10% off regular-priced menu items and merchandise at participating Oberlin businesses on the day of this event by showing your registration confirmation email. Get more details.

Access Statement:

Limited accessible parking is available near the back entrance to the home. Download a map to the accessibility parking at the house. This historic home is partially accessible to wheelchairs and fully accessible to most rolling walkers. The presentation space is wheelchair accessible and limited seating is available. The restroom is not wheelchair accessible, nor gender specific. Listening devices and ASL are currently not scheduled for this event, nor will it be recorded.

If you are interested in touring the home outside of scheduled open houses, groups of 10 or more may make arrangements by contacting the Education Department at education.amam@oberlin.edu.

event.title: Frank Lloyd Wright Open House / June-November 2026
event.ticket_cost: $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18
event.description_text:
Designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, the Weltzheimer/Johnson House is the first Usonian house in Ohio, and one of the few in the nation open to the public. Situated a mile and a half from the art museum at 534 Morgan Street in Oberlin, the house is open to the public the first Sunday of the month (with the exception of holiday weekends) from April to November with a docent presentation offered on the hour at noon, 1, 2, 3, and 4 p.m.

Advance registration is required. Admission to the house is $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18. For tickets, parking information, and other details, see amam.oberlin.edu/flw.

Make a day of it!
The Allen Memorial Art Museum is open 1–5 on Sundays, and is always free. Plus, enjoy 10% off regular-priced menu items and merchandise at participating Oberlin businesses on the day of this event by showing your registration confirmation email. Get more details.

Access Statement:

Limited accessible parking is available near the back entrance to the home. Download a map to the accessibility parking at the house. This historic home is partially accessible to wheelchairs and fully accessible to most rolling walkers. The presentation space is wheelchair accessible and limited seating is available. The restroom is not wheelchair accessible, nor gender specific. Listening devices and ASL are currently not scheduled for this event, nor will it be recorded.

If you are interested in touring the home outside of scheduled open houses, groups of 10 or more may make arrangements by contacting the Education Department at education.amam@oberlin.edu.

The restroom is not wheelchair accessible, nor gender specific. Listening devices and ASL are currently not scheduled for this event, nor will it be recorded.

If you are interested in touring the home outside of scheduled open houses, groups of 10 or more may make arrangements by contacting the Education Department at education.amam@oberlin.edu.

event.title: Frank Lloyd Wright Open House / June-November 2026
event.ticket_cost: $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18
event.description_text:
Designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, the Weltzheimer/Johnson House is the first Usonian house in Ohio, and one of the few in the nation open to the public. Situated a mile and a half from the art museum at 534 Morgan Street in Oberlin, the house is open to the public the first Sunday of the month (with the exception of holiday weekends) from April to November with a docent presentation offered on the hour at noon, 1, 2, 3, and 4 p.m.

Advance registration is required. Admission to the house is $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18. For tickets, parking information, and other details, see amam.oberlin.edu/flw.

Make a day of it!
The Allen Memorial Art Museum is open 1–5 on Sundays, and is always free. Plus, enjoy 10% off regular-priced menu items and merchandise at participating Oberlin businesses on the day of this event by showing your registration confirmation email. Get more details.

Access Statement:

Limited accessible parking is available near the back entrance to the home. Download a map to the accessibility parking at the house. This historic home is partially accessible to wheelchairs and fully accessible to most rolling walkers. The presentation space is wheelchair accessible and limited seating is available. The restroom is not wheelchair accessible, nor gender specific. Listening devices and ASL are currently not scheduled for this event, nor will it be recorded.

If you are interested in touring the home outside of scheduled open houses, groups of 10 or more may make arrangements by contacting the Education Department at education.amam@oberlin.edu.

event.title: Philosophy Talk: Nonfiction Is Not (Just) to Be Believed
event.description_text:
The Oberlin College Philosophy Department is excited to welcome Assistant Professor Hannah H. Kim of the University of Arizona for her lecture entitled "Nonfiction Is Not (Just) to Be Believed."

Philosophers of fiction routinely rely on an unexamined contrast class: nonfiction. The dominant assumption is that nonfiction is discourse meant to be believed (while fiction is discourse meant to be imagined or make-believed). This paper argues that this assumption about nonfiction is unsatisfying. Focusing on memoir and art criticism, I show that central nonfiction genres are not governed primarily by norms of belief. If nonfiction is not unified by an expectation or invitation to believe, then the make-believe/belief contrast cannot mark the boundary between fiction and nonfiction. We therefore need a more nuanced account of what, if anything, unifies nonfiction, if only to provide a more stable foundation for theorizing about fiction.
